title: Condition: to check how many people are chosen
excerpt: How can I create a workflow where I check: if two people (doesn’t matter who) are chosen, then notify the creator of the item, to chose only one

posts.author: Viktoriya (Viktoriya)
posts.cookedHtml: How can I create a workflow where I check: if two people (doesn’t matter who) are chosen, then notify the creator of the item, to chose only one

posts.author: Megan345 (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Viktoriya, have you got some more details about your use case?

If you’re using a People column you can directly restrict the number of people who can be assigned:

posts.author: Viktoriya (Viktoriya)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you very much, but users choose two on a form, if I set up as you show, there is an error but the message is just: “Form submit failed. Try again later”. Is it possible to forbid to choose two on the form?

posts.author: Megan345 (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ah, I see!

I also get the same result trying it on a form. I don’t think there’s a way to prevent more than one person being initially selected, this could be a good one to submit to monday.com as feedback! For now you could add an explanatory note for your users?

posts.author: Megan345 (Megan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Or alternatively, if you don’t have to use a People column, you can limit selection with a multi-select column? And if you still need a person assigned, set an automation?

posts.author: Viktoriya (Viktoriya)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you very much for your help, I will try the first case: to write the explanatory note for users
